# `*.blueprint.ron` — field reference

<!-- GENERATED by `cargo run -p dc_data_docs` (dronecom #2815). Do not edit: field docs live as /// comments on the Rust structs. -->

Data: [`blueprints/`](../blueprints/)

## `Blueprint`

A fully configured vehicle design — the editable source a commander edits
in the designer. Nested items reference the library by `BlueprintId`; the
materialized spawn-time form is `dc_types::BlueprintRecipe`.

### `id` : [`BlueprintId`](#blueprintid)

Stable unique identifier (a UUID) for this design in the library. Not
included in `content_hash`, so two designs with identical content still
carry distinct ids.

### `codename` : [`Codename`](#codename)

Free-text name the player types (e.g. "Lynx"); combined with
`designator` in `full_name` (e.g. "MQ-220 Lynx").

### `designator` : [`Designator`](#designator)

Free-text military-style type designator the player types (e.g.
"MQ-220"); combined with `codename` in `full_name`.

### `chassis_id` : [`ChassisId`](#chassisid)

Identifier of the chassis definition this design is built on; resolves
to a `ChassisDefinition` in the registry.

### `fuel_tank_percent` : f32

Fraction of available space allocated to the fuel tank (0.0–1.0).

### `inventory` : `Vec`<[`InventoryItem`](#inventoryitem)>

Ordered list of mounted components and carried nested blueprints.
Private: read via `Blueprint::inventory` and mutate through the
dedicated methods (`add_component`, `set_inventory`, …) so
`content_hash` stays consistent.

### `doctrine_setting` : [`BlueprintDoctrineSetting`](#blueprintdoctrinesetting)

How this blueprint's doctrine is determined. `Suggested` auto-tracks
capabilities via `dc_blueprints::doctrine::suggested_blueprint_doctrine`;
`Manual(...)` stores a user-authored override. Persisted in the RON.

### `trajectory` : [`TrajectoryProfile`](#trajectoryprofile)

Default trajectory profile the vehicle launches with. Must be a member
of the chassis's `supported_trajectories` (validated in `validate_recipe`
for `FixedWing` chassis). Persisted in the RON; threaded onto the spawn
`BlueprintRecipe` via `materialize_recipe`.

### `default_altitude` : [`DefaultAltitude`](#defaultaltitude)

Role-level default altitude posture the vehicle launches with. Persisted
in the RON; threaded onto the spawn `BlueprintRecipe` via `materialize_recipe`.

## `BlueprintId`

Unique identifier for a blueprint (UUID v4).

### `0` : `Uuid`

The underlying UUID (v4).

## `Codename`

Human-readable codename (e.g. "Lynx", "Gale").

### `0` : `Arc`<str>

The codename string (e.g. "Lynx").

## `Designator`

Military-style designator (e.g. "MQ-220", "TF-340").

### `0` : `Arc`<str>

The designator string (e.g. "MQ-220").

## `ChassisId`

Identifier for a chassis definition, used as a registry lookup key.

### `0` : `String`

The chassis definition's registry key string.

## `InventoryItem`

An item in a blueprint's inventory list.

### `kind` : [`InventoryItemKind`](#inventoryitemkind)

What occupies this inventory slot — a component, a nested blueprint, or fuel.

## `BlueprintDoctrineSetting`

How a blueprint's doctrine is determined.

`Suggested` means "auto-track capabilities" — the cached effective
value on `BlueprintStats` is recomputed from the suggested rules every
time the blueprint changes. The variant carries no payload; the value
is derived, not stored.

`Manual` means "user-set" — the stored value is the doctrine, period.
Editing the blueprint's components leaves it alone (and may fire
warnings via `validate_blueprint_doctrine`).

Variants:

- **`Suggested`** — Auto-tracked from the blueprint's capabilities — the effective doctrine is recomputed from the suggested rules whenever the blueprint changes.
- **`Manual`**([`BlueprintDoctrine`](#blueprintdoctrine)) — User-set — the stored `BlueprintDoctrine` is the doctrine and editing the blueprint's components leaves it unchanged.

## `TrajectoryProfile`

How a missile shapes its vertical path from launch to target.

Variants:

- **`Direct`** — Fly straight at the target (baseline behaviour).
- **`TerrainFollow`** — Follow the terrain: hold clearance above max(ground, sea level), sampled at the vehicle XZ — hugs ridges over land, skims over water. High drag, short range, late detection.
- **`Lofted`** — Powered climb → cruise high in thin air → glide on a gravity arc after burnout → terminal dive. The single long-range profile: cruise altitude scales with launch range (farther targets loft higher), so a long shot banks more altitude and trades it back for downrange in an unpowered best-glide once the motor runs dry. Subsumes the former Ballistic profile.

## `DefaultAltitude`

A blueprint's default altitude posture at launch (role-level). Seeds the
vehicle's `AltitudeCommand`; the player/AI can change it afterward via the
usual `SetCommandedAltitude` directive.

`Ceiling` / `TerrainFollow` are air-only (validated at recipe load). Surface
chassis must be `Chassis` (resolves to `AltitudeCommand::NotApplicable`).

Variants:

- **`Chassis`** — Inherit the chassis `default_altitude` as a `Hold` — the legacy behavior.
- **`Ceiling`** — `Hold` at the chassis `max_altitude` (the operating ceiling). Air only.
- **`Hold`**(f32) — `Hold` at a specific MSL. Validated against the chassis envelope.
- **`TerrainFollow`** — Nap-of-the-earth masking (`AltMode::TerrainFollow`); retained MSL = chassis default. Air only.

## `InventoryItemKind`

What occupies an inventory slot.

Variants:

- **`Component`** — A component from a component definition.
  - `component_id` : [`ComponentId`](#componentid) — Identifier of the component definition mounted in this slot.
- **`Blueprint`** — Nested blueprint, referenced by library `BlueprintId`.
  - `id` : [`BlueprintId`](#blueprintid) — Library `BlueprintId` of the nested blueprint carried in this slot.
- **`Fuel`** — Fixed-quantity fuel order. Cost and quantity come from config.

## `BlueprintDoctrine`

Player-authored subset of doctrine, stored on `Blueprint` and cached
on `BlueprintStats`. The other 3 runtime `Doctrine` fields
(`engagement_range`, `safety_range`, `auto_rtb`) are derived from
capabilities at spawn time and are not authored per blueprint.

### `posture` : [`Posture`](#posture)

Standing combat posture — how aggressively the unit reacts to threats.

### `roe` : [`RulesOfEngagement`](#rulesofengagement)

Rules of engagement — which contacts the unit may fire on (hold fire,
known enemies only, or enemies and unknowns).

### `emcon` : [`EmconLevel`](#emconlevel)

Emissions-control level — whether the unit's active sensors may radiate
(Passive keeps them silent, Active enables emitters).

## `ComponentId`

Identifier for a component definition, used as a registry lookup key.

### `0` : `String`

The component definition's registry key string.

## `Posture`

How far a unit will leave its mission to fight.

Variants:

- **`Pursue`** — Engage permitted hostiles with no range limit (chase beyond the mission anchor), then resume the mission.
- **`Defend`** — Engage permitted hostiles within the mission's engagement zone and the self-defence floor near the unit; do not chase beyond the leash.
- **`Evade`** — Do not engage; flee threats within `safety_range`; resume the mission when clear.

## `RulesOfEngagement`

Target discrimination — which contacts the entity may engage.

Variants:

- **`HoldFire`** — Do not engage any contacts.
- **`EngageKnown`** — Engage contacts classified as Enemy.
- **`EngageUnknown`** — Engage contacts classified as Enemy or Unknown.

## `EmconLevel`

Emissions control — whether active sensors are allowed to emit.

Variants:

- **`Passive`** — Disable all active sensors.
- **`Active`** — Enable all sensors including active emitters.
